title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Jackson County Sheriff)

communications.subject: Oregon Public Records Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Jackson County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Oregon Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

communications.subject: FW: EXTERNAL: Oregon Public Records Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Jackson County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
Good afternoon-

Jackson County is in receipt of your public records request below received on 1/28/26.  The County is the custodian of records.  We have begun working on reviewing our records to determine an estimate for your request.

In an initial review of the responsive records, it has been determined that we do have a spreadsheet of data, similar to what you requested.  However, it is likely not a complete set of data as the agency was not fully operational in the software throughout the time period you are looking for.  It does contain the majority of the data categories you are looking for, but not all of them.  The cost to provide this set of information would be significantly less than also pulling all individual reports.  For comparison, but not a hard estimate, we can likely provide the spreadsheet data in the hundreds of dollars range, compared to the thousands of dollars range for tracking down every individual report responsive to the request.  Again, this is general and only after a cursory review.
Would you be interested in an estimate for the spreadsheet data available or would you like us to continue to provide a full estimate of all data requested for the 9 year time period specified?

Please let me know so we can proceed further.

Sincerely,

Katie

Katie Zerkel
Senior Assistant County Counsel
Jackson County Counsel Office
10 S. Oakdale Ave. Room 214
Medford, OR 97501
Office: 541-774-6162 / Fax: 541-774-6722
Zerkelkm@jacksoncountyor.gov<mailto:Zerkelkm@jacksoncountyor.gov>

title: Facilities Construction and Procurement Records (2023–Present)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Facilities Construction and Procurement Records (2023–Present)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records maintained by Alma Fuerte Public School for the period January 1, 2023 through the present relating to construction, renovation, repair, or facilities work performed on school property, including work performed with or without a formal contract, and regardless of dollar amount.

Specifically, I am requesting:

- Contracts, agreements, memoranda of understanding, work authorizations, or informal arrangements for construction, renovation, repair, or facilities-related work performed on campus, including contracts under $5,000 that are maintained internally by the school.

- Procurement and payment records, including invoices, purchase orders, reimbursements, receipts, stipends, or any other records reflecting compensation or consideration for facilities or construction work.

- Contractor or worker documentation, including business names, individual names, contractor license numbers, proof of license, insurance certificates, bonding documentation, or records reflecting whether work was performed by licensed or unlicensed individuals.

- Internal approvals or authorizations, including administrative approvals or governing board approvals, for construction, renovation, repair, or facilities work.

- Permits, inspection reports, or compliance documentation associated with any construction, renovation, or facilities projects.

- Email correspondence or written communications between school administrators, staff, or governing board members regarding construction, renovation, repair, facilities work, or procurement related to campus improvements.

This request seeks copies of existing records only and does not require the creation of new records.
If any portion of this request is denied or redacted, please identify the specific exemption relied upon.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Steve Bennett

communications.subject: The Missouri Sunshine Law Request: Joplin's Flock Meetings
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the The Missouri Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

I request all material related to meetings of any kind held about or mentioning Flock, Flock Safety, or Flock Group as a significant topic.  These materials should include: agendas, minutes, and electronic calendar records held about private and public meetings on the topic. The search for these materials should include dates from 01/2023 to the date this request is processed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

Sincerely,

Michael

communications.communication:
May I take the invoice being only for Asst. Police Chief time to mean that there are no responsive records in other parts of the city?

I question why four hours of attention from the Asst. Police Chief are needed to find these documents.  Surely there will be outlook records that can point them out very quickly.  I would imagine a search for them using the Outlook Admin portal would produce them quite rapidly, as I have used it this product before.  Can you further explain the nature of the records and the research time given these statements?

I await your response.

title: INCIDENT DOCUMENTATION Title: Incident Documentation - Wallingford Public Schools

communications.subject: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request: INCIDENT DOCUMENTATION Title: Incident Documentation - Wallingford Public Schools
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of Information Act (C.G.S. § 1-200 et seq.), I respectfully request access to the following public records:

PRIMARY RECORDS REQUESTED:
- All incident reports, security logs, or communications from April 1, 2024 to present involving John Conrad, Emelia Conrad, Sage Conrad, or the Conrad family
- Documentation of school access policies and their implementation, including visitor protocols
- Any communications with law enforcement agencies regarding school security matters involving the Conrad family from January 1, 2023 to present
- Records of any safety concerns, threat assessments, or protective measures discussed regarding school personnel or visitors from January 1, 2024 to present

COMPREHENSIVE DISCLOSURE REQUIREMENTS:
This request includes, but is not limited to:
- All responsive records regardless of format (paper, electronic, audio, video, social media, messaging apps)
- Records stored on personal devices if used for official business
- Records in possession of contractors, consultants, or third parties acting on behalf of the agency
- Draft documents, notes, calendars, and informal communications
- Records that have been deleted but remain recoverable
- Metadata and audit logs associated with electronic records

SEARCH REQUIREMENTS:
- Search all relevant custodians, not just those specifically named
- Search all email accounts, shared drives, and backup systems
- Include records of employees no longer with the agency
- Search alternative spellings, nicknames, and partial names

PRODUCTION STANDARDS:
- Provide records in native electronic format when possible
- Do not convert searchable documents to non-searchable formats
- Preserve all metadata including timestamps and sender/recipient information
- Provide a privilege log for any claimed exemptions

If any responsive records have been destroyed, please provide:
- Documentation of the destruction
- Reason for destruction
- Date of destruction
- Authority for destruction

If you claim any exemption under the Freedom of Information Act, please:
- Identify the specific statutory exemption
- Explain how the exemption applies to each document
- Provide redacted copies where partial disclosure is possible
- Consider whether public interest outweighs any claimed exemption

This request seeks records that serve the public interest in government transparency and accountability. Please construe all exemptions narrowly and err on the side of disclosure.

Please preserve all potentially responsive records immediately upon receipt of this request to prevent spoliation.

As these records serve significant public interest, please waive all fees. If fees cannot be waived, please provide detailed cost estimate before proceeding.

Thank you for your assistance.

I also request that, if possible and pursuant to Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-212(d), fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest and not made for commercial gain. The requested documents will be made available to the public at MuckRock.com.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 4 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

john Conrad

title: WSP Officer Rosters

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: WSP Officer Rosters
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I request any electronic spreadsheets maintained by the Washington State Patrol that show the following for all current and former sworn employees of the WSP, year by year, dating back as far as data are maintained:

• Name
• Badge number(s)
• Employee ID
• CJTC ID number
• Date of hire
• Rank/job title
• Date of separation, if applicable
• Reason for separation, if applicable
• All assignments within WSP (e.g., office, section, other assignments) and dates of assignments/assignment changes

This request seeks electronic data under RCW 42.56.010.

Invisible Institute is an award-winning nonprofit public accountability journalism organization based in Chicago. Our mission is to enhance the capacity of citizens to hold public institutions accountable. Because I, the requestor, am a journalist, and I seek the information in order to disseminate it to the public, and because the information regards the safety, welfare, and legal rights of the general public, I request a fee waiver for this data under RCW 42.56.120(4). It is not for commercial benefit because I am a member of the nonprofit news media whose principal purpose is to access and disseminate information regarding the general public’s safety, welfare, and legal rights. To the extent you intend to assess any charges, please notify me to discuss first.

Thank you for your time and prompt attention to this matter. Please contact me should you have any questions related to this request.

Sam Stecklow
Invisible Institute
Experimental Station
6100 South Blackstone Avenue
Chicago, Illinois 60637

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Sam Stecklow

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2106816/” Chapter 42.56 RCW governs disclosure of public records in Washington.  The Public Records Act is generally construed liberally to promote access to public records.  See RCW 42.56.030.  However, RCW 42.56.070(9) specifically prohibits the disclosure of "lists of individuals requested for commercial purposes".  This section "does not distinguish between different types of commercial purposes".  AGO 1998 No. 2, at 3; see also AGO 1975 No. 15. 

Your public records request is for a list of individuals, with additional information about each individual.  RCW 42.56.070(9) prohibits state agencies from providing “lists of individuals requested for commercial purposes.”   Prior to providing you with the information you requested, please complete and return the Declaration of Non-Commercial Purpose form loaded to the portal. 

In order to confirm that this request is not for commercial purposes, please also provide the purpose for which this list will be used.  “An agency has an obligation to investigate (and) at least require a party requesting a list of individuals to state the purpose of the request.”  (SEIU 775 v. DSHS and Freedom Foundation Div II 2016) 

Please log in to the Public Records Center at the below link to retrieve the Declaration. Please note that this link will only remain active for 30 days. Public Records Request - R015130-101325 Once your completed declaration is returned, we will be able to proceed with your request.  If the completed declaration is not received within 30 days, we will consider this request abandoned and close it.  Should you have any questions or to view the full details of this request please log into the Public Records Center.  Sincerely, Den Declet, Forms and Records Analyst 3

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Leon County Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Leon County Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

January 29, 2026 
 RE: Public Records Request R036198-012926 
 
Dear Samuel Sinyangwe: 
This letter acknowledges receipt of your public records request dated January 29, 2026 for the following items: 
 Describe Records Requested: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records: 

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days. 

Sincerely, 

Samuel Sinyangwe 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2201930/ 
 Type of Records Requested: Other 
 Type of Detention Records Requested: 
 Involved Party Detail: 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
If this list does not accurately reflect the item(s) you requested, please contact us immediately at (850) 606-3317 for clarification. To ensure a proper and timely response, your request will be processed in the order in which it was received. It is the policy of the Sheriff's Office to process all requests as quickly as possible. If you provided contact information, we will contact you when your request is completed. If you did not provide contact information, please call (850) 606-3317 for the status of your request. 
 NOTE: The Identity or name of sexual battery victims, child abuse victims, elder abuse victims, 911 callers, Marsy's Law claim, misdemeanor juvenile arrests, spouses and children of law enforcement officers, firefighters, and other statutorily protected occupations are exempt from release or confidential per Florida Statutes: sections 365.171(12), 394.4615(1), 985.04(1), 119.071(4), 119.071(2)(h) and 415.107(1). Thus, we are unable to provide records for these based on a name search, as doing so would identify them by name. 
If you should have any further questions, please do not hesitate to contact us at (850) 606-3317. Please reference Request Number R036198-012926 in all communications. 
 Please note: Under Florida's Public Records laws, most written communications to or from Sheriff's Office staff or officials regarding Sheriff's Office business are public records available to the public and media upon request. Your communications and/or 119 Public Records request may, therefore, be subject to public disclosure. 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you'll receive an email when your request has been completed.

Good afternoon.  Our internal affairs unit has provided me with data from 2017 thru 2024.  However, it isn't consistent for each year.  Some are spread sheets and some are annual reports.  There have been three different programs for Response to Resistance reports during this time period.  I can provide this information, or I can start pulling the yearly reports.  I'm uncertain how many reports there are in total but there are fees associated with review and redaction.  We bill 100 pages per staff hour.  One staff hour is $28.38 per hour.  This could very well be hundreds of dollars.  Let me know if you wish to review the annual reports and datasheets that are available. title: Face recognition agreements, training, and use materials (Baltimore Police Department)

communications.subject: Maryland Public Information Act Request: Face recognition agreements, training, and use materials (Baltimore Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

This is a public records request being submitted under Maryland Public Information Act.

I hereby request the following records related to this agency’s use of any face recognition platform, including but not limited to any system over which this agency does not maintain primary ownership but to which members of the agency have access or any platform that enables face recognition:

1. AGREEMENTS and CONTRACTS
Any and all agreements (including related amendments, attachments, and exhibits) related to the acquisition and use of this "software," algorithm, or program.

2. INSTRUCTIONAL MATERIALS and TRAINING
All instructional materials, presentations and presentation materials and other guidance on the use of "the software." Responsive materials include recorded video and audio, PowerPoint files, prepared remarks, and slides. This also includes training material governing the use, sharing, or access to the "software" or any data related to or collected by the face recognition software/technology, including the legal standard that is required before using the technology.

3. PRIVACY ASSESSMENTS
A copy of any data retention guidelines or policies or other materials evaluating or guiding the security of the "software" or the privacy of the data involved.

4. USE POLICIES 
Please provide a copy of any policy directives, guidance documents, memoranda, training materials, or similar records governing the use and function of this technology for any law enforcement purpose. Responsive materials include all those related to data retention, permissible and impermissible use, and security standards.

5. USE LOGS
All logs documenting the use of and searched conducted using the face recognition system for the period from August 1, 2025 through February 23, 2026. 

This is a request being made in the public interest and not for commercial motives.

If your office anticipates that it will need to charge fees for the processing of this request, please inform me of the estimated costs to fulfill this request in advance of fully processing this request.

Please provide these materials to me electronically, if possible.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response in accordance with the dictates of the public records law.

Sincerely,

Patrick File

title: Boulder County Flock ALPR

communications.subject: RE: Colorado Open Records Act Request #R000116-011526
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Colorado Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. ALPR Locations: Please provide the precise location of each Flock Safety ALPR camera deployed and utilized by Boulder County Sheriff's Office.
2. Email Communications with Flock Safety (specifically emails sent to and received from email addresses ending in “@flocksafety.com”): All metadata(from address, to address(es), cc address(es), bcc address(es), date/time sent, date/time received, subject line, and names of all attachments. This request does not seek the contents of the emails themselves, only the specified metadata.) of email communications between Boulder County Sheriff's Office and Flock Safety between January 1, 2021 and the present.
3. Hotlist Criteria: Please provide documentation of policies detailing the criteria and procedures for creating and verifying entries on hotlists in Flock Safety's ALPR system. Specifically:
a. Criteria for adding entries to hotlists (e.g., offense types, jurisdictional warrants, etc.)
b. Verification and approval processes before hotlist alerts are actionable
4. Data-Sharing Agreements: List of all agencies with whom Boulder County Sheriff's Office shares ALPR data, both within Colorado and out-of-state, including federal entities.
5. Policies on ALPR Use and Oversight: Any formal policies or guidelines related to:
a. Acceptable and prohibited uses of ALPR data, including specific restrictions and permissible use cases
b. Access policy, including what constitutes a “valid reason” for system access
c. Human verification procedures for hotlist alerts before law enforcement action
6. Access Logs and Audits: Any access logs, audits, compliance reports, or oversight records evaluating the use, accuracy, and policy compliance of Boulder County Sheriff's Office ALPR systems between January 1, 2021 and the present.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Matthew Spratlin

communications.subject: Records Request :: R000116-011526
communications.communication:
Dear MATTHEW SPRATLIN: 
We appreciate your interest in criminal justice records of the Boulder County Sheriff’s Office. Your request was received on  1/15/2026 and will be processed according to the Colorado Criminal Justice Records Act (CCJRA), C.R.S. 24-72-301 et seq., and other applicable laws. Disclosure of records involving children may be restricted by the Children’s Code Records and Information Act, C.R.S. 19-1-301 et seq. 
The Sheriff’s Office Records Section does not process requests for public records under the Colorado Open Records Act (CORA), C.R.S. 24-72-201 et seq. Requests for public records under CORA should be submitted to the Boulder County’s Open Records Center available at https://bouldercounty.gov/records/colorado-open-records-act/. (https://u8387778.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.VEsWrlMiJDg0mXafq1mEXaWhAOeQS4p-2FTifjFiHmIBToUhCNwPrijCljaecY3mINRUTFikS2djaQyg-2FJ7hIZ8luPa63HTgmuk3H1tWjGbKI-3DQW5S_Y6hkOFzc7Gmm3-2Bi7zo-2BZvuu4uIOBSkIp7jMmXKFRuYHJRlwdqXH86OpLg0WOws0MTIxkdrrxCuNEvvOPj2QMFlM-2FT-2FZ7glL-2BBqYg9N4wYHZ8lWuxVeAa-2B7P3ulgRA8fuExOEDO32UGNLt8gJiNNi-2FTmlOXJMMM5C6bP7PaJw-2F7l8wABoXl3wkVYCMFqPMD8UbKoFrnP9oUF-2FgVIN352e055NnrnUqbc7SpPbnSvla8Q-3D) 
You will receive an email when the response to your request is ready. While the Records Section is working diligently to respond, please note that the 3-day timeframe for inspection under CORA does not apply to requests for criminal justice records under the CCJRA. This request can be tracked at the link below using assigned reference number R000116-011526. 
The Sheriff’s Office also publishes certain criminal justice information online, including daily booking and listing reports for the Boulder County Jail, available at: https://bouldercounty.gov/safety/jail/listing-and-booking-reports/. (https://u8387778.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.VEsWrlMiJDg0mXafq1mEXaWhAOeQS4p-2FTifjFiHmIBT2MJBJQmvdwUDiv0zYVq10kivwqXhRz5ZRo0NzoQrJNkh6yFBa-2FP7KQLm7eFSs948-3DpP8T_Y6hkOFzc7Gmm3-2Bi7zo-2BZvuu4uIOBSkIp7jMmXKFRuYHJRlwdqXH86OpLg0WOws0MTIxkdrrxCuNEvvOPj2QMFmKLNulZn-2FRuB2-2FuXNkTTOzLJFJlIucpmrnUfewSM76Sk5iGKQX7kfEvKZzeodUz-2B28lQGCUaemLEQi91br7cP1GeS8gScJHiYoQgC9C-2FbVNq9vqZJ2XXtctyOajwSoXHsSeZs8Sfzf9ox88G5zqe3A-3D)

Thank you, Boulder County Sheriff's Office

communications.subject: CORA Request :: C000121-031026
communications.communication:
Dear Matthew Spratlin: 
Thank you for your interest in the public information of Boulder County. Your request was received on 3/10/2026 and given the reference number C000121-031026 for tracking purposes. 
Records Requested: Email Communications with Flock Safety (specifically emails sent to and received from email addresses ending in "@flocksafety.com"): All metadata(from address, to address(es), cc address(es), bee address(es), date/time sent, date/time received, subject line, and names of all attachments. This request does not seek the contents of the emails themselves, only the specified metadata.) of email communications between Boulder County Sheriffs Office and Flock Safety between January 1, 2021 and the present. 

Submitting this separate request per the Sheriff's Office response: 
RESPONSE: This request for email communications should be submitted to the Boulder County Open Records Center, which you can access at the following link: https://bouldercountyco.govqa.us/WEBAPP/_rs/(S(ztdsrhglvuapgleza4ghawiq))/supporthome.aspx 
You will be contacted about the availability of records and/or provided with copies of the records. Depending on the scope of your request, a deposit and/or extension may be requested. If a deposit and/or extension is needed, Boulder County will contact you with an estimate before proceeding. All communications will be through the Boulder County Records Center. PLEASE NOTE: The Colorado Open Records Act (CORA) does not require Boulder County to create new records, to do legal research, or to answer questions. 
You can monitor the progress of your request under " My Requests" in the Boulder County Open Records Center.

title: Missing Person Jonathan Hoang (Police Department)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Missing Person Jonathan Hoang (Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Given the case of missing person Jonathan Hoang (Case Number S025-42596) has been determined "no evidence of foul play" and therefore, not under criminal investigation, the following documents are requested for public review to aid in the search to find this vulnerable young man:
1. Any incident report, narrative, or similar report concerning the endangered missing persons case of Jonathan Hoang.
2. Any police or investigator notes, including interviews with neighbors, school teachers/administrators, or family members.
3. Any notes, memos or meeting recordings or communications within and across law enforcement, federal, or government agencies (including communications with Kirkland Police Department, WA state FBI, WA state senators) related to the Jonathan Hoang case.
4. Any recordings: bodycam footage, vehical camera footage, video recordings related to the Jonathan Hoang case. 
5. Any notes, memos or meeting recordings related to communications with the Hoang family.
The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Elin Bjorling

title: City of Roanoke Rescue of Black-and-White Tuxedo Cat (Valiente)

communications.subject: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: City of Roanoke Rescue of Black-and-White Tuxedo Cat (Valiente)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

February 21, 2026

 FOIA Officer for the City of Roanoke, Virginia
robin.viohl@roanokeva.gov

Re: FOIA Request – October 27, 2024 Rescue of Black-and-White Tuxedo Cat (Valiente)

Dear FOIA Officer:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I request records related to the rescue, handling, and custody of a black-and-white tuxedo cat named Valiente at Towers Shopping Center, Roanoke, Virginia, on October 27, 2024. This request includes records in the custody of both the Roanoke Police Department (including Animal Control) and Roanoke Fire-EMS.
Please provide all records, electronic or paper, in the following categories:

1. Personnel Information
Names, badge numbers, and assignments of any Police officers present at the scene.
Any Animal Control officers notified, dispatched, or involved.
Names and assignments of Fire-EMS personnel, including Battalion Chief Robert Perdue and the responding firehouses.

2. Incident and Dispatch Records
CAD reports, dispatch logs, event summaries, and call detail records related to the incident.
Incident reports, after-action reports, or any internal reports generated by Police or Fire-EMS in connection with this event.

3. Body-Worn Camera / Audio / Video
All Police body-worn camera footage, dash camera footage, and radio communications/audio logs related to the rescue.
Any Fire-EMS audio, video, or operational recordings capturing the rescue or related communications.

4. Animal Handling / Custody Records
Intake logs, custody forms, or reports for Valiente on or about October 27, 2024.
Records showing which personnel or units had custody or transported the cat after the rescue.
Any documentation showing transfer to RCACP, veterinary providers, or other entities, including timing and personnel involved.
Records reflecting release, adoption, euthanasia, or continued custody of the cat.

5. Communications
Emails, memoranda, or written communications referencing Valiente.
Communications between Police, Fire-EMS, RCACP, or veterinary providers regarding the rescue, custody, or evaluation of the cat.

6. Metadata / Additional Notes
If records exist electronically, please provide metadata showing creation, modification, or access, to the extent easily available.
Please first confirm which records exist and provide an estimate of any fees before producing records, so that I may consider narrowing the request if necessary.
If separate fees apply for Police or Fire-EMS records, please notify me prior to processing.
If portions of records are exempt from disclosure, please identify the statutory exemption and provide all reasonably segregable portions.

Please note that during the incident, I may have used a different phone number than my current one; records associated with that number are responsive to this request.

Thank you for your assistance in fulfilling this request.

Respectfully, 

/s/ Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr.

Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr., pro se
1328 Peachtree Street NE
Atlanta, Georgia 30309-3209
(404) 389-5642
csdelozier@pm.me

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr..

communications.subject: RE: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: City of Roanoke Rescue of Black-and-White Tuxedo Cat (Valiente)
communications.communication:
Subject: FOIA Request Follow-Up – Submitted February 21, 2026

Dear FOIA Officer,

I am following up on my Freedom of Information Act request submitted on February 21, 2026, via the MuckRock portal, regarding the rescue of Black-and-White Tuxedo Cat (Valiente).

Under Virginia Code § 2.2‑3704(B), public bodies are required to respond to FOIA requests within five working days, or within an extended timeframe if a written extension is properly issued. As of today, no official acknowledgment or response has been received through the MuckRock portal or other formal FOIA channels.

Please be advised:

Any correspondence sent outside the official FOIA submission channel (including private email) does not constitute a statutory acknowledgment or response.

This request is now past the statutory response timeframe. Failure to provide a proper written response may be treated as a deemed denial under Virginia law, which could give rise to formal legal action, including a petition in circuit court to compel compliance.

I respectfully request that your office provide a written acknowledgment and any required records or fee estimate immediately. Timely compliance will ensure that this request proceeds in accordance with Virginia law and is properly documented for use in related legal matters.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

Sincerely,

/s/ Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr., pro se

communications.subject: RE: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: City of Roanoke Rescue of Black-and-White Tuxedo Cat (Valiente)
communications.communication:
FOIA Request – Payment and Fire-EMS Records

Dear Ms. Harper,

Thank you for providing the cost estimate to fulfill my FOIA request regarding the rescue of Valiente on October 27, 2024. I would like to proceed with the request and cover the estimated fees. Could you please advise the preferred method of payment? For example, is there a portal for electronic payment, or should I submit a check or money order?

I also want to clarify the inclusion of Fire-EMS records. As noted in my request, multiple Fire-EMS units responded to the incident, including three fire trucks. Could you confirm which records exist for the responding personnel, units, and operational activities, and whether any additional fees apply for producing these records? My intent is to obtain all relevant documentation regarding the rescue and handling of Valiente on that date.

Thank you for your assistance, and I look forward to your guidance on completing this request.

Sincerely,

/s/ Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr.

communications.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL] RE: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: City of Roanoke Rescue of Black-and-White Tuxedo Cat (Valiente)
communications.communication:
On March 2, 2026, you made a request for records in accordance with the Virginia Freedom of Information Act (VFOIA) wherein you requested various records related to a black-and-white tuxedo cat, Valiente,  and in incident at Towers Shopping Center, Roanoke, Virginia, on October 27, 2024. On March 6th, I advised of the costs associated with this request. You responded after close of business on March 9th, agreeing to the costs and advising the City to proceed with your request.

The responsive records from Roanoke City Fire-EMS (Fire-EMS), the Roanoke City Police Department (RPD), and E911 have been identified and are provided at this time. However, I am still working with the Animal Warden/Animal Control to determine if that Department has any records responsive to your request. Accordingly, it is practically impossible to provide the requested records, or determine whether they are available, within the five working days required by FOIA due to staffing and the time needed to compile and review responsive records. Therefore, the City invokes § 2.2-3704(B)(4) providing an additional seven working days to respond to your request. The deadline for the City to respond to your request is March 19, 2026.

Please see the City’s responses, in bold italics, in response to your requests below.

1. Personnel Information
Names, badge numbers, and assignments of any Police officers present at the scene.
Any Animal Control officers notified, dispatched, or involved.
Names and assignments of Fire-EMS personnel, including Battalion Chief Robert Perdue and the responding firehouses.

City’s Response to No. 1.
The names and assignments (position/rank) of the responding Fire-EMS and RPD personnel are contained in the NFIRS Report and Incident Details Report attached. Please be advised that the employee specific ID numbers have been redacted as that information is exempt from mandatory release pursuant to Virginia Code § 2.2-3705.1(1).
*Animal Warden/Animal Control records TBD.


2. Incident and Dispatch Records
CAD reports, dispatch logs, event summaries, and call detail records related to the incident.
Incident reports, after-action reports, or any internal reports generated by Police or Fire-EMS in connection with this event.

City’s Response to No. 2.
Please see the NFIRS Report and Incident Details Report attached in response to No. 1.
*Animal Warden/Animal Control records TBD.


3. Body-Worn Camera / Audio / Video
All Police body-worn camera footage, dash camera footage, and radio communications/audio logs related to the rescue.
Any Fire-EMS audio, video, or operational recordings capturing the rescue or related communications.

City’s Response to No. 3.
Please see the body-worn camera footage provided to you directly by the Roanoke Police Department. No additional video or audio from Fire-EMS, RPD, or E911 exist in response to your request.
*Animal Warden/Animal Control records TBD.


4. Animal Handling / Custody Records
Intake logs, custody forms, or reports for Valiente on or about October 27, 2024.
Records showing which personnel or units had custody or transported the cat after the rescue.
Any documentation showing transfer to RCACP, veterinary providers, or other entities, including timing and personnel involved.
Records reflecting release, adoption, euthanasia, or continued custody of the cat.

City’s Response to No. 4.
*Animal Warden/Animal Control records TBD.


5. Communications
Emails, memoranda, or written communications referencing Valiente.
Communications between Police, Fire-EMS, RCACP, or veterinary providers regarding the rescue, custody, or evaluation of the cat.

City’s Response to No. 5.
No responsive records from Fire-EMS, RPD, or E911 exist in response to your request.
*Animal Warden/Animal Control records TBD.



6. Metadata / Additional Notes
If records exist electronically, please provide metadata showing creation, modification, or access, to the extent easily available.

City’s Response to No. 6.
Please see responsive data, easily available, shown on the NFIRS and Incident Details Report attached in response to No. 1.
*Animal Warden/Animal Control records TBD.


Jessica Harper
Executive Administrative Assistant
FOIA Officer
Office of the City Attorney
City of Roanoke

title: Enforcement of Plate/Tab Laws during Metro Surge - Hopkins PD

communications.subject: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: Enforcement of Plate/Tab Laws during Metro Surge - Hopkins PD
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Minnesota Government Data Practices Act, I hereby request the following records:

In light of recent public reporting and video documentation showing federal immigration personnel operating vehicles in the Twin Cities without visible license plates, with expired registration tabs, or in apparent violation of Minnesota vehicle statutes, I am seeking data to better understand how your agency enforces these laws uniformly within its jurisdiction, including whether such statutes are applied consistently regardless of the driver’s federal affiliation. The information we are requesting is not available on your Community Crime Map. We are requesting:

1. Summary Enforcement Data

Please provide a monthly summary (January 1, 2025 through present) of citations and warnings issued for the following violation types:
- Expired registration tabs
- No license plates
- Improperly displayed plates
- Switched or fictitious plates
- Operating a vehicle without valid registration

These may include (but are not limited to) the following statutes: Minn. Stat. § 168.09 – Registration / Display of Registration Plates, Minn. Stat. § 168.04 – Display of License Plates, Minn. Stat. § 169.79 – License Plates; Display Requirements, Minn. Stat. § 168.12 – Registration Tax / Tabs

For each statute listed above, please provide:
- Total citations issued (monthly totals)
- Total warnings issued (monthly totals - if available)
- Whether citation resulted in arrest (Y/N summary)
- Any internal code used for these violations
- Please do NOT requesting any personally identifiable information
----------

2. Stops or Citations Involving Federal Law Enforcement Personnel

For the same date range, please provide:
- The number of traffic stops conducted by Hopkins PD involving drivers identified as federal law enforcement personnel (if tracked)
- The number of citations or warnings issued to federal law enforcement personnel for the statutes listed above
- The number of such stops that resulted in no citation or warning
- If identity of agency (e.g., DHS, ICE, FBI) is tracked in any field, please include aggregate totals by agency.
- No names or personal identifying data are requested.

If no responsive records exist, please confirm that fact.
----------

3. Policies and Guidance

Please provide copies of:
- Current Hopkins PD written policies regarding enforcement of vehicle registration and license plate violations.
- Any written policy, directive, or standing order addressing enforcement discretion for government-owned or federal vehicles.
- Any temporary directives or operational guidance issued during Operation Metro Surge that address traffic enforcement priorities. This may include patrol briefs, SOPs, or other temporary directives issued regarding enforcement of plate/tab laws. Please include any memos, emails, powerpoint slides, or other documentation regarding temporary guidance for these vehicle violations since Operation Metro Surge began. 

Thank you in advance for the assistance. If any portion of this request is denied, please cite the specific statutory exemption relied upon. Let me know if I can provide additional context to aid in your search.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Alan J. Segal

title: Virginia State University Board of Visitors

communications.subject: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Virginia State University Board of Visitors
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All records of correspondence between members of Virginia States University's leadership, its Board of Visitors, any member of the Virginia State Legislature or the VMI Board of Visitors or administration from the Period 1 May 2025 to present on the subject of dissolving the VMI Board of visitors and moving governance of VMI under VSU.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Anonymous.

communications.subject: RE: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Virginia State University Board of Visitors
communications.communication:
Good Evening:

This message acknowledges receipt of your email.

Pursuant to Virginia Code § 2.2 - 3704 (A), FOIA is available to "citizens of the Commonwealth, representatives of newspapers and magazines with circulation in the Commonwealth, and representatives of radio and television stations broadcasting in or into the Commonwealth." Accordingly, please provide a copy of your driver's license with a verified Virginia address.

Kind regards,

VSU FOIA Administrator

title: Virginia State University Policies

communications.subject: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Virginia State University Policies
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. A copy of VSU Student Honor Code/Code of Ethics covering lying, cheating, stealing, plagiarism, etc.  
2. A copy of VSU Student Honor/Code of Ethics System detailing how violations of the Honor Code/Student Code of Ethics relating to lying, cheating, stealing, plagiarism, etc. are reported, charged, adjudicated with the range of sanctions and who receives reports and conducts the adjudication.
3. ROTC Commissioning rates by service from 2019 to present by race and gender.
4. Admissions policies, including any diversity goals or objectives by race and gender.
5. Copy of VCU's DEI policy and trainings, as well as whether student attendance is required.
6. Admissions data, including applications, acceptances, enrollment by race and gender from AY 2019 - 2020 - Present
7. Student retention data by class, race, and gender, from AY 2019 - 2020 - Present
8. Amount of funding received from the Virginia State University Alumni Association, VCU Foundation or related alumni groups, funds and/or endowments in total and as a percentage of the University's overall funding from AY 2019 - 2020 - Present
9. Any current concept papers or planning documents, reports or other matrials on the subject of the VSU BOV taking over governance f the Virginia Military Institute

The requested records are reasonably believed to be immediately available within existing records and, therefore, should be provided within the 5-day statutory requirement under the Virginia FOIA.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Anonymous.

communications.subject: RE: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Virginia State University Policies
communications.communication:
Good Evening:

This message acknowledges receipt of your email.

Pursuant to Virginia Code § 2.2 - 3704 (A), FOIA is available to "citizens of the Commonwealth, representatives of newspapers and magazines with circulation in the Commonwealth, and representatives of radio and television stations broadcasting in or into the Commonwealth." Accordingly, please provide a copy of your driver's license with a verified Virginia address.

Kind regards,

VSU FOIA Administrator

communications.subject: RE: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Virginia State University Policies
communications.communication:
Good afternoon VSU Adminstrator, 

Thank you for bringing this matter to my attention. Please see the attached, thank you!

communications.subject: RE: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Virginia State University Policies
communications.communication:
Good Evening:

This office is in receipt of your request for records made in accordance with the Virginia Freedom of Information Act (Virginia Code § 2.2-3700 et seq.). Please be advised that it is practically impossible to provide the requested records or determine whether they are available within the five (5) work days required by FOIA because of time demands associated with other University operational obligations. Therefore, the University is invoking subsection B 4 of Virginia Code § 2.2-3704 to provide an additional seven (7) work days to respond to your request.

Please also be advised that pursuant to Virginia Code § 2.2-3704 (F), the public body may make reasonable charges "in accessing, duplicating, supplying, or searching for requested records." Accordingly, a cost estimate is forthcoming.

Thank you for contacting this office.

Kind regards,

VSU FOIA Administrator

Please see the attached.

Kind regards,

VSU FOIA Administrator

title: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (secretary of state)

communications.subject: North Dakota Open Records Law Request: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (secretary of state)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the North Dakota Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

From January 20, 2025 to the present:

1. All correspondence, emails, letters, and written communications between the agency and the U.S. Department of Justice, including but not limited to the Civil Rights Division, the Office of the Attorney General, and the Executive Office for Immigration Review, relating to voter registration data, voter files, voter rolls, or voter list maintenance.

2. All internal communications within the agency (including emails, memoranda, and briefing materials) discussing, referencing, or responsive to any DOJ request for voter registration data or any proposed or executed memorandum of understanding with the DOJ regarding voter data.

3. Any lists, spreadsheets, databases, or other records received from the DOJ identifying specific voters for removal, investigation, or further review.

4. All records relating to the engagement of any third-party contractor in connection with DOJ voter data requests or voter list maintenance activities conducted pursuant to any agreement with the DOJ.

If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please identify the specific statutory exemption relied upon for each withholding.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen

title: Public Records Request – Five Below, Inc. (Post-Inspection Communications)

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: Public Records Request – Five Below, Inc. (Post-Inspection Communications)
communications.communication:
Pursuant to the Michigan Freedom of Information Act, I request copies of all public records held by the Michigan Department of Agriculture and Rural Development from November 1, 2025 to present relating to Five Below, Inc., other than routine inspection reports already produced.

Specifically, this request seeks:

Any communications (emails, letters, memoranda, notes) between MDARD staff and Five Below, Inc. or its representatives following inspection findings

Any communications between MDARD and the Michigan Department of Attorney General referencing Five Below, Inc., Scanner Law compliance, or post-inspection status

Any internal MDARD communications discussing Five Below’s compliance, remediation, persistence, or repeat inspection outcomes

This request is not intended to duplicate previously produced inspection reports, but to identify any related communications or records created outside of the inspection reports themselves.

If no such records exist, please confirm that fact in writing.

Records in electronic format are requested where available.

communications.subject: RE: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request #791LAB60465
communications.communication:
Please note:
A delivery date was not provided; what was provided was an estimate. It was also for business, not calendar days.  In the original response dated February 9, 2026, we wrote that “We estimate the Department will complete the processing of your request within 10 business days. Section 4(8) of the FOIA, MCL 15.234(8), provides that the time frame estimate is nonbinding to the public body, but the public body shall provide the estimate in good faith and strive to be reasonably accurate, and provide the public records in a manner based on this state’s public policy set forth in section 1(2) of the FOIA, MCL 15.231(2), and the nature of the request in the particular instance “

In the spirit of cooperation, we are currently working on your request. The responsive records have been retrieved and are currently being reviewed for exempt material. When they are ready, we will provide them.


Brad Deacon
Freedom of Information Act Coordinator
Michigan Department of Agriculture
and Rural Development

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Lafourche Parish Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: Louisiana Public Records Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Lafourche Parish Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Louisiana Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: Audio Recording(s) of Edward Felt

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Audio Recording(s) of Edward Felt
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1). Copies of all the audio recording(s) collected by the FBI regarding the phone call(s) made to the Westmoreland County 911 Center, Westmoreland County Department of Public Safety in Greensburg, PA that were collected in response to the Flight 93 hijacking on September 11, 2001. It's publicly reported by the FBI that Flight 93 passenger Mr. Edward Felt called from his cell phone to the Westmoreland County 911 Center and that the FBI is in possession of the audio recording of the call that took place. 

2). Any and all phone carrier logs or written communications that the FBI collected from Flight 93 passenger Edward Felt cell phone provider, if any. 

3). Any and all written interview records involving this particular subject regarding the phone call from the Flight 93 passenger Edward Felt.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Charles Kallas

that took place. 2). Any and all phone carrier logs or written communications

the statute requires. Sincerely, Charles Kallas Upload documents directly:

**

Please be advised that efoia@subscriptions.fbi.gov is a no-reply email
address. Questions regarding your FOIA request may be directed to
foipaquestions@fbi.gov. If you have received a FOIPA request number, please
include this in all correspondence concerning your request. Please note eFOIPA
requests are processed in the order that they are received. If you have not
received a FOIPA request number, your request is in the process of being
opened at which time it will be assigned a FOIPA request number and
correspondence will be forthcoming.

**

Upon receipt of your FOIPA request number, you may check the status of your
FOIPA request on the FBI’s electronic FOIA Library (The Vault) on the FBI’s
public website, http://vault.fbi.gov by clicking on the ‘Check Status of Your
FOI/PA Request tool’ link. Status updates are performed on a weekly basis. If
you receive a comment that your FOIPA request number was not located in the
database, please check back at a later date.

title: West Virginia DOC Youth Room and Board + Medical Copay Fee Information

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: West Virginia DOC Youth Room and Board + Medical Copay Fee Information
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

The following materials regarding the imposition, assessment, collection and balances of fees and debt for incarcerated youth (i.e., juvenile):

a) All current policies and procedures regarding medical and room & board costs or fees for which incarcerated youth (i.e. juvenile) and/or their parents or legal guardians are or may become liable. Please include all policies and procedures related to how fees are set, accrued, imposed, collected, and enforced (such as those related to per diem fees, ability to pay determinations, penalties for late or non-payment, authorization for civil or criminal action for payment enforcement, etc).

b) All reports, databases, spreadsheets, or equivalent materials regarding medical copays and room and board fees paid to or owed to this department/office or any facility/agency under its jurisdiction in FY2024. Please include all relevant data regarding medical and room & board costs, including the number of youths (i.e., juveniles) charged for medical and room & board costs or fees and the total amount of medical and room & board fees imposed in FY2024.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jenny Wadhwa

title: Request on Political Appointees (Food and Drug Administration)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Request on Political Appointees (Food and Drug Administration)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

This is a request under the Freedom of Information Act. I hereby request the following records:

1. A list of any and all political appointments at the agency appointed from January 20, 2025 until the date of your final response to this request.

2. Copies of resumes for any and all political appointees hired since January 20, 2025 until the date of your final response to this request. These records are likely in the possession of the Designated Agency Ethics Official (DAEO).

Please provide these records as available on a rolling basis.

Documented is an investigative watchdog and reporting group that explores the relationship between special interests and government. This request is likely to contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of the operations of the government. Disclosure of the requested records serves the public interest, and this request is not being made for any commercial purposes. Therefore, I am requesting a waiver of any fees. If you do not grant my fee waiver request, please notify me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request be filled electronically by email attachment or other electronic file transfer if available, or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter.  I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,
Nina

communications.subject: Request FDA-FOIA-2025-8406 Response
communications.communication:
March 9, 2026 
Sent Via Email: 189191-19548289@requests.muckrock.com 
Subject: FOIA Request FDA-FOIA-2025-8406 
Dear Requester: 
The Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has completed processing your September 23, 2025, Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request, in which you sought. To Whom It May Concern: This is a request under the Freedom of Information Act. I hereby request the following records: 1. A list of any and all political appointments at the agency appointed from January 20, 2025 until the date of your final response to this request. 2. Copies of resumes for any and all political appointees hired since January 20, 2025 until the date of your final response to this request. 
We are providing you with copies of the records responsive to your request. However, portions of the records have been withheld under the following exemptions: 5 U.S.C. § 552 (b)(2), which protects records "related solely to the internal personnel rules and practices of an agency." (https://www.govinfo.gov/content/pkg/USCODE-2011-title5/html/USCODE-2011-title5-partI-chap5-subchapII-sec552.htm) 5 U.S.C. § 552 (b)(6), which protects information about individuals in "personnel and medical files and similar files" when the disclosure of such information "would constitute a clearly unwarranted invasion of personal privacy. (https://www.govinfo.gov/content/pkg/USCODE-2011-title5/html/USCODE-2011-title5-partI-chap5-subchapII-sec552.htm) 
Additionally, the following sections of the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) and FDA FOIA regulations are applicable to this partial denial. 45 C.F.R. § 5.31(b), which authorizes our agency to withhold records that are related solely to the internal personnel rules and practices of an agency. (https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-45/part-5#p-5.31(b)) 45 C.F.R. § 5.31(f), which authorizes our agency to protect information in personnel and medical files and similar files when the disclosure of such information would constitute a clearly unwarranted invasion of personal privacy. (https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-45/subtitle-A/subchapter-A/part-5#p-5.31(f)) 21 C.F.R. § 20.63, which protects personnel, medical, and similar files, disclosure of which constitutes a clearly unwarranted invasion of personal privacy. (https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-21/chapter-I/subchapter-A/part-20#p-20.63(b)) 21 C.F.R. § 20.66, which allows us to withhold from public disclosure records related solely to the internal personnel rules and practices of the FDA. (https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-21/section-20.66) 
In determining to withhold such information, FDA considered 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(8)(i), when applicable, and whether FDA reasonably foresees that disclosure of such information would harm an interest protected by the relevant exemption(s) and whether disclosure is prohibited by law. 
In accordance with 45 CFR § 5.61 and 21 CFR § 20.41(b)(5), you have the right to appeal this determination.  By filing an appeal, you preserve your rights under FOIA and give the agency a chance to review and reconsider your request and the agency’s decision. Your appeal must be mailed or emailed within 90 days from the date of this response, to: 
Director, Office of Management and Enterprise Services 
US Food & Drug Administration 
5630 Fishers Lane, Room 1050 
Rockville, MD 20857 
E-mail: FDAFOIA@fda.hhs.gov (mailto:FDAFOIA@fda.hhs.gov) 
Please clearly mark both the envelope and your letter “FDA Freedom of Information Act Appeal.” Your appeal should also reference your request number FDA-FOIA-2025-8406-OHR-1. Items arriving or delivered after 5 p.m. Eastern Time will be deemed received on the next workday. 
If you would like to discuss our response before filing an appeal to attempt to resolve your dispute without going through the appeals process, please feel free to contact me at 240-402-9116. You may also contact the FDA FOIA Public Liaison for assistance at: Office of Management and Enterprise Services, US Food & Drug Administration, 5630 Fishers Lane, Room 1050, Rockville, MD 20857, E-mail: FDAFOIA@fda.hhs.gov. (mailto:FDAFOIA@fda.hhs.gov) 
If you are unable to resolve your FOIA dispute through our FOIA Public Liaison, the Office of Government Information Services (OGIS), the Federal FOIA Ombudsman’s office, offers mediation services to help resolve disputes between FOIA requesters and Federal agencies. OGIS can be contacted at: 
Office of Government Information Services 
National Archives and Records Administration 
8601 Adelphi Road–OGIS 
College Park, MD 20740-6001 
Telephone 202-741-5770 
Toll free 1-877-684-6448 
Facsimile at 202-741-5769 
E-mail at ogis@nara.gov. (mailto:ogis@nara.gov) 
Sincerely yours, 
Charis Wilson, PhD, CRM 
Denials & Appeals Officer 
Division of Headquarters Freedom of Information

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Requesting documents of ALL Open/Closed/Sustained Internal Affairs investigations between January 2023- December 2025 for all federally employed personnel within their respected departments.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Candace B Cupples

title: Keel's Consulting Records

communications.subject: The Missouri Sunshine Law Request: Keel's Consulting Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the The Missouri Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Invoices, contracts, bills, and purchase orders relating to the company "Keel's Consulting" for the years 2025 and 2026. If electronic, I request these documents in their original format, rather than being converted to alternate formats before production.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Michael

communications.subject: Re: Public Records Request No. 11 - Keel's Consulting Records
communications.communication:
Dear MuckRock News:

This letter acknowledges receipt of your public records request for the invoices, contracts, bills, and purchase orders relating to the company "Keel's Consulting" for the years 2025 and 2026.

We are currently reviewing the extent of this records request for its propriety under the applicable law and determining whether any responsive documents are available. Upon completion of our review, we will be in touch regarding next steps.

Very truly yours,

Will Peterson

communications.subject: Re: Public Records Request No. 11 - Keel's Consulting Records
communications.communication:

The City of Seneca received your public records request dated March 4, 2026, pursuant to Chapter 610 of the Missouri Revised Statutes (the "Sunshine Law"), seeking invoices, contracts, bills, and purchase orders relating to the company "Keel's Consulting".
Enclosed with this email, please find the City's responsive records.
This production completes the City's response to Public Records Request No. 11. If you have any questions or concerns regarding this response, please do not hesitate to contact me directly.
Very truly yours,
William E. Peterson
Wood & Peterson, LLC
217 S. Lafayette Street
Neosho, MO 64850
