This message, including attachments, is from the law firm of Wood & Peterson, LLC. This message contains information that may be confidential and protected by the attorney-client or attorney work product privileges. If you are not the intended recipient, promptly delete this message and notify the sender of the delivery error by return email or by telephone at 417-451-4141. You may not forward, print, copy, distribute, or use the information in this message if you are not the intended recipient.
PLEASE NOTE: The Missouri Bar Disciplinary Counsel requires all Missouri lawyers to notify all recipients of e-mail that (1) e-mail communication is not a secure method of communication, (2) any e-mail that is sent to you or by you may be copied and held by various computers it passes through as it goes from me to you or vice versa, (3) persons not participating in our communication may intercept our communications by improperly accessing your computer or my computer or even some computer unconnected to either of us which the e-mail passed through. I am communicating to you via e-mail because you have consented to receive communications via this medium. If you change your mind and want future communications to be sent in a different fashion, please let me know AT ONCE.

title: GenPrime Fertility

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: GenPrime Fertility
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

GenPrime Fertility has had a Los Angeles location since 2024.

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records (dated from January 1, 2024 to the time this request is processed):
- All tips and 911 audios/transcripts made from the address 1808 W Lincoln Ave, Anaheim, CA 92801, or mentioning said address. Please include any subsequent CAD reports, incident reports, and investigative documents produced in connection to these calls.
- All body camera footage produced in any police responses to the address 1808 W Lincoln Ave, Anaheim, CA 92801.

I respectfully request that my request is expedited, given the newsworthiness and high-profile nature of abuse of the US fertility industry. This WSJ article highlights the issue: https://www.wsj.com/us-news/chinese-billionaires-surrogacy-pregnancy-7fdfc0c3. It mentions that Peter Thiel's family office "has backed a chain of IVF clinics across Southeast Asia and a recently opened branch in Los Angeles." This is GenPrime Fertility. I also respectfully request that documents are released on a rolling basis.

I am a news reporter for WIRED (wired.com), so the requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. Therefore, I request that any fees are waived. But in the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by email attachment if available.

If you need to contact me, you can reach me at caroline_haskins@wired.com, or here. Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,
Caroline Haskins

title: Mark Anthony Conditt and Austin Bombing (Federal Bureau of Investigation)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Mark Anthony Conditt and Austin Bombing (Federal Bureau of Investigation)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

In 2018 a series of bombings were perpetuated by Mark Anthony Conditt. The investigation involved the FBI, Austin Police Department, and the Texas Department of Public Safety. I am requesting 

- Audio or direct transcription of the 25 minute video recording my Mark Anthony Conditt as referenced by Governor Greg Abbot and former Austin Police chief Brian Manley -
 https://web.archive.org/web/20180323120234/https://www.statesman.com/news/breaking-austin-bombing-suspect-dies-police-close-official-says/KZmUAGvKlNazDr31EzeUzI/
https://www.washingtonpost.com/news/morning-mix/wp/2018/03/22/austin-bomber-challenged-young-man-or-terrorist/

- Any documents relating to the investigation, identification, or pursuit of Mark Anthony Conditt

- Any documents regarding a follow up investigation on any groups or associates of Mark Anthony Conditt. 

I ask that the fee be waived as this information is within the public interest and many documents were widely commented on despite them not being available to the public (namely the 25 minute recording) release and access of this information is crucial to fair and accurate reporting on this matter.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Nate Zaffiro

title: Peter J. Peters

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Peter J. Peters
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records relating to, mentioning, or generated as a result of investigation into Peter John Peters (November 13, 1946 – July 7, 2011), who led the LaPorte Church of Christ in Laporte, Colorado until his death. Peters used the church to promote white supremacist ideology. Proof of death is not required as his death was widely reported: https://www.splcenter.org/hatewatch/2011/07/15/influential-christian-identity-pastor-dies

Please conduct a search of the Central Records System, including but not limited to the Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) Indices, the Microphone Surveillance (MISUR) Indices, the Physical Surveillance (FISUR) Indices, and the Technical Surveillance (TESUR) Indices, for both main-file records and cross-reference records for all relevant names, individuals, agencies and companies. This search is also to include any responsive records held in offsite locations, such as the Alexandria Records Center. Please include both investigatory and non-investigatory records in your search. If any potentially responsive records have been destroyed and/or transferred to NARA, then I request copies of the destruction or transfer slips as well as any other documentation relating to, mentioning or describing said transfer or destruction, to include but not be limited to confirmation that the Bureau has no other copies of said records. If previously released records are available, then I request a rolling release consisting of those records while additional records are located and processed for release.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Paul Galante

communications.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL EMAIL] -  RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #1553307-000
communications.communication:
Thank you for contacting foipaquestions@fbi.gov.  Please check the status of your FOIPA Request at http://vault.fbi.gov by clicking on “Check Status of Your FOI/PA Request” on the right side of the page, and follow the instructions below.

Check the Status of Your FOIPA Request
If your FOIPA Number is [1234567-0] please enter [1234567-000] into the system. If your FOIPA Number is [1234567-1] please enter [1234567-001] into the system. If you have any questions about the status of your FOIPA request, please e-mail foipaquestions@fbi.gov.

FIND STATUS OF FOIPA- Request statuses are updated weekly

Please enter the whole FOIPA number-Example: [1234567-000]

FOIPA:
Results will show the Request Number, Case Type and Process Description shown below:
FOIPA:
1234567-000

Case Type:
FOIPA

Process Description (Will display the current progress of the request)
The FBI’s FOIPA Program is searching the FBI’s indices for potentially responsive documents.
You may be contacted via formal letter for all fees and/or negotiation issues that may apply.

NOTE: Recent requests are entered into the FOIPA database in the order that they are received. Before you can check the status, you must have received correspondence assigning a FOIPA request number and the information transferred to the online database. Status information is updated weekly. If a request has been closed within the last six months the online database will display the following: The FOIPA number entered has been closed, and appropriate correspondence has been sent to the address on file.

Estimated Dates of Completion
Requests are processed in the order in which they are received through our multi-track processing system.  Requests are divided into two primary tracks--simple (under 50 pages of potentially responsive documents) and complex (over 50 pages of potentially responsive documents).  Complex requests are further divided into medium, large, and extra-large sub-tracks based upon request size.  Simple track requests typically require the least amount of time to process.  Currently, simple track cases average approximately 98 days from the date of receipt for processing.  Our complex requests in the medium processing track are currently averaging 947 days, large processing track are currently averaging approximately 2,011 days, and extra-large processing track are currently averaging 2,360 days for processing.

Respectfully,

Public Information Officer
FBI - Information Management Division
200 Constitution Drive
Winchester, VA  22602
O: (540) 868-4593
E: foipaquestions@fbi.gov<mailto:foipaquestions@fbi.gov>

Do you have further questions about the FOI/PA process? Visit us at http://www.fbi.gov/foia

Please check the status of your request online at https://vault.fbi.gov/fdps-1/@@search-fdps    Status updates are performed on a weekly basis.

Note:  This is a non-emergency email address.  If this is an emergency, please call 911 directly.  If you need to report a tip for immediate action, please contact FBI Tips at http://tips.fbi.gov/ or reach out to your local field office.

title: VESSEL REGISTRATION APPLICATION for HULL ID AOV37411A324 Registured to John Gunter (Delaware Fish And Wildlife Boat Registration Office)

communications.subject: Delaware Freedom of Information Act Request: VESSEL REGISTRATION APPLICATION for HULL ID AOV37411A324 Registured to John Gunter (Delaware Fish And Wildlife Boat Registration Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Delaware Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Hello, I am looking for a copy of the VESSEL REGISTRATION APPLICATION for HULL ID AOV37411A324, registered to John Gunter. 

Not worried about any fees being charged, I will gladly pay them!

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 15 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jonathan Rudenberg, a citizen of Delaware, in coordination with Kyle L'Hommedieu.

title: TXFC: Free USA Foundation (СVободные Штаты Америки) Telegram Account and Austin Protest Call

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: TXFC: Free USA Foundation (СVободные Штаты Америки) Telegram Account and Austin Protest Call
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am seeking access to and copies of records maintained by the Texas Department of Public Safety (DPS) and the Texas Fusion Center (TXFC) related to a specific report of potential foreign influence and propaganda activities. 

Background Information

On or about September 29, 2022, a tip was submitted to the Texas Fusion Center (TXFC@dps.texas.gov) by a Robert "Robby" Delaware regarding a Russian-language Telegram account named "Free USA Foundation" (СVободные Штаты Америки). The report detailed efforts by this account to recruit protesters for a gathering at Austin City Hall on September 30, 2022, and highlighted the account's use of pro-Kremlin rhetoric and "Thin Blue Line" imagery to incite civil discord in Texas. 

One image of this account was preserved online via the Internet Archive:

https://web.archive.org/web/20220929131546/https://t.me/freeusafoundation/18

Original URLs:
https://t.me/freeusafoundation
https://t.me/freeusafoundation/18

Records Requested
I respectfully request the following records created, received, or maintained between September 20, 2022, and the present:

Communications & Internal Logs: Any internal emails, logs, or notes from the Texas Fusion Center or DPS personnel regarding the tip received from Robert "Robby" Delaware on September 29, 2022.

Intelligence Products: Any bulletins, "Situational Awareness" alerts, "Blue Notes," or intelligence summaries generated by the TXFC or DPS that mention the "Free USA Foundation," "СVободные Штаты Америки,", the specific URL of https://t.me/freeusafoundation or the proposed Austin City Hall protest on September 30, 2022.

Third-Party Correspondence: All correspondence between the Texas Department of Public Safety/Texas Fusion Center and Telegram Messenger Inc. (or its legal/compliance departments) regarding the removal or investigation of the "Free USA Foundation" accounts.

Inter-Agency Shared Info: Any communications with the FBI or the City of Austin (including Austin Police Department) regarding this specific Telegram network and its attempts to organize in Texas.

If certain parts of this request or certain portions must be redacted please provide any reasonable segregable portion of a requested record after removing or redacting those portions claimed to be exempt, explain in writing the justification for redacting the record, indicate the extent of any redactions on the portion of the record which is made available or published and where technically feasible indicate on the redacted portion itself the specific exemption(s) claimed.

Thank you to the Texas Fusion Center for the Center's commitment to transparency and for the work the center does every day.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of February 17, 2026., Reference # P004305-021726. 
Dear Robert Delaware, 
The Texas Department of Public Safety received a public information request from you on February 17, 2026. Your request mentioned: "I am seeking access to and copies of records maintained by the Texas Department of Public Safety (DPS) 
and the Texas Fusion Center (TXFC) related to a specific report of potential foreign influence and 
propaganda activities." 
DPS has located records responsive to your request; however, we believe these records may be excepted from required public disclosure at this time.  DPS is seeking a ruling from the Office of the Attorney General (OAG) with respect to disclosure of these records, and a copy of our request letter is attached.  You will be notified directly by the OAG when a ruling is issued. 
If you have any questions or need additional information, please submit your response through this portal. 
Sincerely, 
ML Calcote 
Sr. Assistant General Counsel 
OGC

title: Teleradiology Services (Immigration and Customs Enforcement)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Teleradiology Services (Immigration and Customs Enforcement)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All invoices, purchases orders, contracts, loan agreements, statements of work, procurement documents (but not limited to solicitation documents or notices of proposed contracts, proposed bids, unsolicited proposals, and/or documents justifying contracting without full and open competition) relating to the following specific procurement identifiers:

Teleradiology_Services

The SAM page can be found here: https://sam.gov/workspace/contract/opp/7be511f74f58439ebd2f131134b3f5ac/view

This request is not being made for commercial purposes. Any documents obtained from this request will be used in the course of reporting for The Latino Newsletter, a new media outlet that focuses on Latinx issues in the United States (https://thelatinonewsletter.org/)

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Carlos Berríos Polanco

communications.subject: Your verification code
communications.communication: Your confirmation code is 104151

communications.subject: ICE FOIA 2026-ICFO-06960
communications.communication:
12/17/2025 

Carlos  Polanco 
MuckRock News, DEPT MR199055 
Boston, Massachusetts 02115 

RE:	ICE FOIA Case Number 2026-ICFO-06960 
 
Dear Requester: 

This acknowledges receipt of your 12/2/2025, Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request to U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), for records pertaining to Teleradiology_Services including all invoices, purchases orders, contracts, loan agreements, statements of work, procurement documents .  Your request was received in this office on 12/2/2025. 

Due to the increasing number of FOIA requests received by this office, we may encounter some delay in processing your request. Per Section 5.5(a) of the DHS FOIA regulations, 6 C.F.R. Part 5, ICE processes FOIA requests according to their order of receipt. Although ICE’s goal is to respond within 20 business days of receipt of your request, the FOIA does permit a 10-day extension of this time period. As your request seeks numerous documents that will necessitate a thorough and wide-ranging search, ICE will invoke a 10-day extension for your request, as allowed by Title 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(6)(B). If you’re able to narrow the scope of your request please contact our office. Narrowing the scope may speed up the search process. We will make every effort to comply with your request in a timely manner. 

Provisions of the FOIA allow us to recover part of the cost of complying with your request.  We shall charge you for records in accordance with the DHS Interim FOIA regulations, as they apply to media requesters.  As a media requester, you will be charged 10 cents per page for duplication; the first 100 pages are free.  We will construe the submission of your request as an agreement to pay up to $25.00. You will be contacted before any further fees are accrued. 

We have queried the appropriate program offices within ICE for responsive records. If any responsive records are located, they will be reviewed for determination of releasability. Please be assured that one of the processors in our office will respond to your request as expeditiously as possible. We appreciate your patience as we proceed with your request.

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #2026-ICFO-06960
communications.communication:
To the ICE Office of the Principal Legal Advisor:

This is an appeal, pursuant to 6 C.F.R. § 5.8, of the final response issued by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) dated [Date of ICE's Final Response Letter], regarding the above-referenced Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request.

On December 2, 2025, I requested records pertaining to “Teleradiology_Services” including all invoices, purchase orders, contracts, loan agreements, statements of work, and procurement documents. ICE’s response, signed by Deputy FOIA Officer Megan Davis, stated that a search was conducted within the “ICE Office of Acquisition Management (OAQ)” and that no responsive records were found.

I appeal this determination on the grounds that the search conducted was inadequate and unreasonably narrow, and therefore violates the requirements of the FOIA.

Inadequate Search Scope: The request seeks records related to contracts, procurement, and financial transactions. The search appears to have been confined to a single office, the Office of Acquisition Management (OAQ). It is not credible that all responsive records—particularly invoices, statements of work, and operational procurement documents—would reside solely in one central contracting office. Responsive records are likely to be held by the specific ICE program office(s) or field offices that utilized or managed the Teleradiology_Services, their corresponding financial or budget offices, the Office of the Chief Financial Officer, and/or the Office of Procurement. The failure to search these other record systems renders the agency’s search legally insufficient.

Request for Expanded Search: I appeal the adequacy of the search and request that ICE conduct a new, reasonable search for responsive records. This search must include, but not be limited to:

The Office of the Chief Financial Officer (OCFO)

The program office(s) responsible for detainee healthcare or medical services

ICE Health Service Corps (IHSC)

The Office of Detention Policy and Planning, or relevant Detention Compliance and Removals operations

Any relevant ICE field offices or detention facilities where such services may have been implemented

Any other ICE component reasonably likely to maintain records concerning the procurement or payment for Teleradiology_Services.

A search limited to the OAQ cannot reasonably be expected to locate all records responsive to this request. The appeal seeks a reversal of the “no records” determination and a remand to the agency to perform an adequate search as required by law.

Sincerely,

Carlos Berríos Polanco

Part 5, ICE processes FOIA requests according to their order of receipt. Although ICE’s goal is to respond within 20 business days of receipt of your request, the FOIA does permit a 10-day extension of this time period. As your request seeks numerous documents that will necessitate a thorough and wide-ranging search, ICE will invoke a 10-day extension for your request, as allowed by Title 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(6)(B). If you’re able to narrow the scope of your request please contact our office. Narrowing the scope may speed up the search process. We will make every effort to comply with your request in a timely manner. 

Provisions of the FOIA allow us to recover part of the cost of complying with your request.  We shall charge you for records in accordance with the DHS Interim FOIA regulations as they apply to non-commercial requesters.  As a non-commercial requester, you will be charged 10 cents per page for duplication; the first 100 pages are free, as are the first two hours of search time, after which you will pay the per quarter-hour rate ($4.00 for clerical personnel, $7.00 for professional personnel, $10.25 for managerial personnel) of the searcher.  We will construe the submission of your request as an agreement to pay up to $25.00. You will be contacted before any further fees are accrued. 

We have queried the appropriate program offices within ICE for responsive records. If any responsive records are located, they will be reviewed for determination of releasability. communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #2026-ICFO-06957
communications.communication:
Hello, 

Dear FOIA Appeals Officer:

This is an appeal under the Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), 5 U.S.C. § 552, and the Department of Homeland Security’s FOIA regulations, 6 C.F.R. Part 5 § 5.8. I appeal the determination contained in the final response letter from Deputy FOIA Officer Megan Davis, dated 12/17/2025, regarding ICE FOIA Case Number 2026-ICFO-06957.

The response stated that a search of the ICE Office of Acquisition Management (OAQ) located no records responsive to my request for records pertaining to 26-SS-DCR-02, including all invoices, purchase orders, contracts, loan agreements, statements of work, and procurement documents. I believe the agency’s search was inadequate and/or the “no records” determination is improper for the following reasons:

Likely Existence of Records: The subject of the request, “26-SS-DCR-02,” appears to be a specific project, contract, or procurement identifier consistent with government naming conventions. It is highly improbable that a search for core procurement documents (contracts, statements of work, purchase orders) related to a named project would yield no results if conducted in the correct record systems and offices.

The SAM page can be found here: https://sam.gov/workspace/contract/opp/8787177db2ae492897540d79f07dfd3f/view

Public Information Suggests Other Custodians: Publicly available information indicates that the office associated with this identifier is DETENTION COMPLIANCE AND REMOVALS. This office is listed as the contracting office for "26-SS-DCR-02" in the federal System for Award Management (SAM.gov). Therefore, it is highly likely that responsive records are in the custody of the DETENTION COMPLIANCE AND REMOVALS program office, not solely OAQ. The search appears to have overlooked this critical custodian.

Narrow Scope of Search: The response indicates a search was conducted only within the “ICE Office of Acquisition Management (OAQ).” While OAQ is a logical place to start, responsive records may also reside with:

The ICE program office or field location that originated or manages the 26-SS-DCR-02 project, specifically DETENTION COMPLIANCE AND REMOVALS.

The ICE Office of the Chief Financial Officer (OCFO) for financial records, invoices, and payment documents.

Other DHS components or federal agencies if the procurement was inter-agency.

ICE’s contract writing or financial management systems (e.g., PRISM, EAGLE), which may not have been adequately searched by querying OAQ’s files alone.

Insufficient Search Description: The response does not describe the search methodology used. It does not specify the databases searched, the search terms used (beyond the identifier “26-SS-DCR-02”), the date ranges covered, or the personnel who conducted the search. Without this detail, I cannot assess the adequacy of the search.

Appeal Request: I respectfully request that the agency:

Conduct a new, comprehensive search for records responsive to my request.

Expand the search beyond OAQ to include all ICE offices and systems likely to maintain procurement, financial, and project records related to “26-SS-DCR-02.” This must specifically include the DETENTION COMPLIANCE AND REMOVALS office, as well as the OCFO and other relevant program offices.

Provide a detailed description of the search(es) conducted in any subsequent response.

If any records are located, process them for release pursuant to FOIA.

If the determination is upheld, provide a written explanation detailing the search methods, locations, and terms used to confirm the non-existence of records.

Enclosed is a copy of the final response letter being appealed. I have marked the envelope “FOIA Appeal” as instructed.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter. Please direct all correspondence regarding this appeal to the address above.

Sincerely,

Carlos Polanco

title: Flock Cameras in the Sedalia, MO

communications.subject: The Missouri Sunshine Law Request: Flock Cameras in the Sedalia, MO
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the The Missouri Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

To the Custodian of Records,

Pursuant to the Missouri Sunshine Law, Chapter 610, RSMo, I am requesting access to and copies of public records related to the installation and use of Flock Safety license-plate reader (LPR) cameras in Sedalia, Missouri.

Specifically, I request the following records for the time period January 1, 2023 to present:
	1.	Contracts, agreements, invoices, purchase orders, or memoranda of understanding between the City of Sedalia, the Sedalia Police Department, or any city agency and Flock Safety.
	2.	Records, proposals, or internal memos discussing the acquisition, approval, placement, or operation of Flock LPR cameras.
	3.	Emails or communications (including emails, attachments, text messages, or letters) between:
	•	City of Sedalia officials
	•	Sedalia Police Department officials
	•	Representatives of Flock Safety
	4.	Maps, logs, or documents showing the planned or installed locations of Flock cameras.
	5.	Usage policies, retention policies, data-sharing agreements, or any documentation describing how Flock LPR data is stored, accessed, shared, or retained.
	6.	Any reports, statistics, or evaluations relating to alerts, hits, or effectiveness of the Flock camera system.

I request these records in electronic format (PDF or native electronic files) if available.

If any portion of this request is denied, please provide the specific legal basis for each denial under Chapter 610, RSMo, and release all reasonably segregable portions of otherwise closed records.

Please inform me of any estimated costs before processing, as required by law.

You may send the records or any correspondence to me at:
Johngeorgiyev@gmail.com
6602215136

Thank you for your time and attention. I look forward to your response within the timeframe required by Missouri law.

Sincerely,
John Georgiyev

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

John Geogiyev

title: Central Hudson Metadata

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Central Hudson Metadata
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

1. Records retention schedules currently in effect for the Department of Public Service/Public Service Commission, particularly those covering consumer complaints, utility oversight, rate cases, and enforcement actions.

2. Database inventories or system documentation describing electronic record systems used to track:

Consumer complaints against regulated utilities
Utility rate case filings and proceedings
Enforcement actions, penalties, or corrective orders
Meter reading accuracy audits or billing investigations


3. Index or log of complaints filed against Central Hudson Gas & Electric Corp. (2020-present), including complaint number, date received, complaint category/type, and resolution status. I am not requesting complaint narratives or consumer PII at this time.

4. Index of public hearings (with dates, locations, and hearing officer names) related to Central Hudson rate cases or service quality proceedings (2020-present), and confirmation of whether audio/video recordings exist for each. I am not requesting audio/video recordings at this time.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

EJ Fox

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the South Carolina Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All contracts, licenses, MOUs, MOAs, data agreements, letters of intent, State of Work documents, Scope of Work documents, and other agreement documents with Flock Safety or Flock Group that were active any time between 1/1/2020 and 10/30/2025.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request be filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days for records more than 24-months-old, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

m

communications.subject: FOIA Paralegal | Office of the City Attorney - FOIA Request FOIA2025-1402: Confirmation
communications.communication:
Dear Requester, 
  
 Your request reference number is Request Number: FOIA2025-1402 and your security key is B9F13FB3. Please have the security key and reference number available when communicating with our staff regarding your request. 
  
 Upon receipt of a written request for records under FOIA, the City will determine if the records are available. The City will send a Determination Letter notifying you of its determination within ten (10) business days for records less than two (2) years old, or within twenty (20) business days for records more two (2) years old. 
  
 Pursuant to S.C. Code Ann. § 30-4-30(B), fulfillment of FOIA requests is subject to fees for search and retrieval, as well as fees for providing copies of documents. 
  
 Search and retrieval time is charged at $20 per hour. 
  
 If search and retrieval time is greater than thirty (30) minutes and/or the number of pages to be produced is greater than ten (10) pages, a DEPOSIT (25% of reasonably anticipated cost for reproduction of the records) IS REQUIRED prior to searching for and/or coping records pursuant to a FOIA request. The balance must be paid before responsive documents are produced. 
  
 If the City determines that the requested records are available and are not exempt from disclosure under FOIA, the records will be furnished within thirty (30) calendar days of the date the deposit is received. If the records are more than two years old, the records will be furnished within thirty-five (35) calendar days of the date the deposit is received. 
  
 If the search/retrieval time is less than half an hour and/or the total number of documents responsive to the FOIA request is ten (10) pages or less, no reproduction fee will be charged. 
  
 If no deposit is required and the City determines that the requested records are available and are not exempt from disclosure under FOIA, the City will furnish the records within thirty (30) calendar days of the date of its Determination Letter. Requested records which are greater than two (2) years old will be furnished within thirty- five (35) calendar days of the date of the Determination Letter. 
  
 If you have not elected to receive responsive documents electronically, please visit the Freedom of Information (FOIA) page for the fee chart. 
  
 Thank you,

FOIA Paralegal | Office of the City Attorney
206 S Main St
Greenville, SC 29602
864-467-4373

communications.subject: RE: FOIA2025-1402
communications.communication:
Re: Request Number: FOIA2025-1402 
  
 Dear m, 
  
 This letter is to inform you that pursuant to S.C. Code Ann. § 30-4-30(C), this office has determined that your above-referenced South Carolina Freedom of Information Act (“FOIA”) request seeks information that is available to public. 
  
 The City charges research time at $20.00 per hour. We anticipate expending: 
  
 Invoice Line Item Details 
 Item Description Quantity Price Amount 
 Purchasing Search/Retrieval Time (per hour) 1 $20 $20.00 
 Z1. First half hour free 1 $-10 $-10.00 
 Search/Retrieval Time (per hour) 1 $20 $20.00 
  
 hour(s) of research time for and Estimated cost: 
  
 $30.00 
  
 The responsive documents can be provided to you electronically at no additional cost, or on a USB drive for an additional cost of $12.00. The City requires a deposit (25% of the estimated total cost of producing the records) afforded by S.C. Code Ann. § 30-4-30(B) prior to providing records. 
  
 The amount of the deposit for this request is $7.50. 
  
 To view and pay the deposit the invoice by debit/credit card, click on the request link: Request Number: FOIA2025-1402 
  
 Once the deposit is received, we will furnish the documents to you within the time allotted by FOIA (within 30 calendar days for records less than 24 months old and 35 days for records 24 months or older) with final payment due at the time of the production. 
  
 Payment in full is required for release of the records. If the deposit is not paid within 30 days of this letter, your request will be closed. 
  
 Please note that any information falling within the personal privacy exemption to FOIA will be redacted pursuant to S.C. Code Ann. § 30-4-40(a)(2). Further, the City is not waiving any other exemption that might be applicable. 
  
 Finally, while we are confident these records are not being sought for purposes of commercial solicitation, FOIA nonetheless requires the City inform you that South Carolina law prohibits a person from knowingly obtaining or using personal information for commercial solicitation as more fully set forth in S.C. Code Ann.§ 30-2-50. 
  
 Sincerely, 
  
 Jindriska Macickova
FOIA Paralegal | Office of the City Attorney
www.greenvillesc.gov
Phone: 864-467-4373

communications.subject: RE: FOIA2025-1402
communications.communication:
Re: Request Number: FOIA2025-1402 
  
 Dear m, 
  
 Please allow this letter to serve as the City of Greenville’s response to above- referenced FOIA. 
  
 Out the outset, the City is required to take reasonable measures to ensure that public records are not used for the purpose of commercial solicitation. South Carolina law provides that it is a crime to knowingly obtain or use personal information contained in a public record for commercial solicitation as more fully set forth in S.C. Code Ann. § 30-2-50(D). 
  
 Please note that any information falling within the personal privacy exemption to FOIA is redacted pursuant to Section 30-4-40(a)(2) of the South Carolina Code of Laws. Further, the City is not waiving any other exemptions that might be applicable. 
  
 
Sincerely, 
  
 Jindriska Macickova
FOIA Paralegal | Office of the City Attorney
www.greenvillesc.gov
Phone: 864-467-4373

Dear Mr. Sanchez, I hope this message finds you well. Thank you for submitting your FOIA request, reference number 2026-00556. Please find attached the formal acknowledgement letter associated with your request. If you have any questions or require further assistance, kindly use the ATF FOIAXpress PAL portal’s correspondence feature to contact our office. Sincerely, 

ATF FOIA Team 
Note: This is not a monitored mailbox and replies to this email will not be seen or responded to. If you have any questions or concerns, please use the ATF FOIAXpress PAL portal’s correspondence feature to contact our office. You can access the portal by creating an account at https://dojatf.secureocp.com/FOIAXpress. Additionally, you can contact the FOIAPublic Liaisons at 202-648-7390 for any further assistance and to discussany aspect of your request. For technical issue with PAL, please email foia-pal-support@atf.gov. Thank you for your understanding.

title: West Fertilizer Company Survey

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: West Fertilizer Company Survey
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All records or reports related to the U.S. Army Engineer Research and Development Center (ERDC) assessment of the West Fertilizer Company explosion damage and the estimate of the equivalent explosive yield of the blast, as well as any ERDC documents cited in the assessment. 

I request that I be properly classified as a news media requester. I am an investigative researcher for Bellingcat, and I am requesting this information in that capacity and request that fees be waived for this request.

Bellingcat is widely recognized for its reporting, and publishing regular features on issues of public interest and importance using open source material.

If you have any questions about my media status, please contact me.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Trevor Ball

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request: West Fertilizer Company Survey
communications.communication:

communications.subject: RE: [Non-DoD Source] RE: Freedom of Information Act Request: West Fertilizer Company Survey
communications.communication:
Good morning,

I apologize for the delay in acknowledgement.  Our FOIA mailbox was apparently not receiving emails but has been resolved.

I have reached out to our laboratories that may have records responsive to your request seeking “All records or reports related to the U.S. Army Engineer Research and Development Center (ERDC) assessment of the West Fertilizer Company explosion damage and the estimate of the equivalent explosive yield of the blast, as well as any ERDC documents cited in the assessment.”

For purposes of processing fees, I have assigned your category as “news media”.

I have moved this request to the top of our processing queue to expedite as quickly as possible due to the delay in receiving the request.

Please let me know if you have any questions.  I will provide an update once I hear back from our labs.

Thank you,
Chris

Paralegal Specialist, Office of Counsel
U.S. Army Engineer Research & Development Center

Vicksburg, MS  39180-6199

title: Department of Defense support for TV documentary series "The Mightiest"

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Department of Defense support for TV documentary series "The Mightiest"
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All documents pertaining to collaboration between the Department of Defense (DoD) and the production of the TV documentary series "The Mightiest" (2022-). Such documents may include but should not be limited to: script notes; production assistance agreements; photographs; and all records of communications between employees of DoD and members of the cast, crew, and production company of "The Mightiest," including memos, notes of meetings and conference calls, emails, and other forms of communication.

"The Mightiest" appears on a list of DoD entertainment industry collaborations released to me through FOIA. The list is attached to this request.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Harford County Sheriff)

communications.subject: Maryland Public Information Act Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Harford County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Maryland Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

Correspondence Message 
Dear Samuel Sinyangwe, 
A new message has been sent regarding request MPIA-26-GQ-000116. From: 
Good morning, 
We received your request for UOF data from 2017 to 2025 under the MD Public Information Act.  We do not have a readily available report that provides all the information you've requested; however, we can provide a Use of Force Report list, which has some of the information you are seeking. 
Redactions were made for sociological information GP 4-330, and under GP 4-351(a) for information used or collected for an investigative report. 
After the $45 payment (reduced from $90/$10 per year requested) is made and I receive notification, you will receive an email from Axon with a link to download the requested files.  If payment is made after my working hours, the email will be sent the following working day. 
Sincerely, 
Michelle Hanks PIA Liaison 
View Request 
Need Help? 
If you have questions or need assistance, please contact hanksm@harfordsheriff.org or (mailto:hanksm@harfordsheriff.org) 410-838-6600 (http://nfhltmxx.r.us-east-1.awstrack.me/L0/tel:410-838-6600/1/0100019c52420957-33f156df-717d-4162-ab08-f753afc11a40-000000/YrcRy4X7T8BZjvSzPe11hWX4Qz8=465)

title: Doug Martin

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Doug Martin
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any police incident reports, 9-1-1 calls connected to incidents involving Doug Martin (DOB: January 13, 1989), including but not limited to in connection to the Oct. 18, 2025 incident in which he died in custody

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Wesley Lowery

communications.subject: Your Oakland, CA public records request #25-10950 has been closed.
communications.communication:
Oakland, CA ************************************************************************ Record request #25-10950 has been closed. The closure reason supplied was: Upon review, at this time the City has determined that the incident described in your request does not meet the definition of a “critical incident” under Government Code sections 7923.610–7923.625. Therefore, the mandatory disclosure provisions applicable to critical incidents do not apply.   In addition, the City has determined that the requested records are exempt from disclosure under the CPRA’s investigatory records and public-interest balancing exemption, Government Code §§ 7923.600, 7922.000. After conducting the required case-specific balancing test, the City has concluded that the public interest served by nondisclosure clearly outweighs the public interest served by disclosure in this instance.   Specifically, disclosure at this time would compromise privacy interests, and interfere with an ongoing investigation. Because the public-interest exception applies, the City is unable to disclose the requested records. Pursuant to Government Code § 7922.540, the individual responsible for this determination is Chief of Police James Beere. ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at Oakland, CA.

Technical support: See our help page

title: City of El Cajon: Photographs of Graffiti Uploaded to "Requests and Issues" section of city website

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: City of El Cajon: Photographs of Graffiti Uploaded to "Requests and Issues" section of city website
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All photographs of graffiti uploaded to the city of El Cajon's website (elcajon.gov). These photographs would have been uploaded via this form: https://www.elcajon.gov/i-want-to/report/requests-and-issues

This is just a request for the images uploaded, not for the identifying information in the form itself. 

Time frame for request: January 1st 2021 to March 1st 2026. 

Please provide any reasonable segregable portion of a requested record after removing or redacting those portions claimed to be exempt, explain in writing the justification for redacting the record, indicate the extent of any redactions on the portion of the record which is made available or published and where technically feasible indicate on the redacted portion itself the specific exemption(s) claimed. There is a public interest in seeing what has been flagged as graffiti by the public to the city of El Cajon. 

Thank you to the city of El Cajon's commitment to transparency.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

communications.subject: El Cajon General Public Request No. PRR26-201, Topic: General / Other
communications.communication: Thank you for contacting the City of El Cajon. We have received your request for information and will begin processing it. A response to this request will be sent in a timely manner as allowed by California Government Code Section 7922.535.  In unusual circumstances, that time period may be extended an additional 14 days. We make every effort to respond to requests for information as quickly as possible. Normal business days are Monday - Thursday, 7:30am - 5:30pm, and every other Friday 8:00am - 5:00pm. For alternate dark Fridays, please check the City calendar available online. To check on the status of your request, return to the Public Records site and click on Lookup Request. *NOTE: At "Lookup Request", enter the tracking number, and click on the Submit button (Enter key will not work). You will be prompted for this tracking number, PRR26-201, along with your email, 206007-64844848@requests.muckrock.com

title: City of Fort Worth ChatGPT | City Attorney

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: City of Fort Worth ChatGPT | City Attorney
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Chat histories of all ChatGPT sessions conducted by city employees on city-owned devices or used in job-related functions in the following departments: City Attorney

The timeframe for this request is 7/1/2025 to the date this request is processed. The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. Please make records available in installments as they are ready to release.

If it’s helpful, please share with respondents the following help article and instructions for exporting ChatGPT histories into a .zip folder:

https://help.openai.com/en/articles/7260999-how-do-i-export-my-chatgpt-history-and-data

1. Sign in to ChatGPT.
2. In the top right corner of the page click on your profile icon.
3. Click Settings.
4. Click Data Controls menu.
5. Under Export Data click Export.
6. In the confirmation screen click Confirm export.
7. You should receive an email with your data.
Note: The link in the email expires after 24 hours
8. Click Download data export to download a .zip file will be downloaded including your chat history in chat.html as well as your other data on file with us

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Bradford William Davis

communications.subject: Emailed Public Records Request :: E000517-102825
communications.communication:
Thank you for contacting the City of Fort Worth.  Your e-mail has been given the reference number E000517-102825 for tracking purposes. Your request will be forwarded to the relevant City departments to locate the information you seek. 
 If you have submitted a Public Information Request: 
 
Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, you will receive communication from the City of Fort Worth within 10 business days informing you of one or more of the following: 
 
 
  * The date the information will be available or a copy of the records sought 
  * A cost estimate/invoice 
  * A letter advising there is no responsive information for your request or that some/all the responsive information may or must be withheld by the City of Fort Worth in accordance with the law 
  * The need for clarification from you. 
Please note your request must ask for records that already exist. The Texas Public Information Act does not require a governmental body to create new information, perform legal research, or answer questions. 
 
Again, thank you for contacting the City of Fort Worth Public Records Center. 
To monitor the progress of or update this request please log into the

title: Pittsburg Email System Records

communications.subject: The Kansas Open Records Act Request: Pittsburg Email System Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the The Kansas Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

The latest invoice the City of Pittsburg has paid for the provisioning of Exchange/Outlook email services.  If this invoice does not reflect the licensing level of the accounts being paid for, I also request a record of licenses the City is subscribed to. I have reason to believe these records exist because the City of Pittsburg sends emails with email headers that appear to reference this service within them.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Michael

title: Data on top employers of SNAP recipients

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Data on top employers of SNAP recipients
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Data on the employers in the state of Washington with more than 100 workers receiving benefits from the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP), commonly referred to as food assistance.

Specifically, I request the following information, if available:
- The name of the employer
- The total number of the employer's workers who are currently receiving SNAP benefits
- The time period this data covers (e.g., the most recent quarter or year for which data is available)

I understand that specific identifying information for individual benefit recipients is protected by privacy laws, and my request is solely for aggregated, non-personally identifiable data regarding employer affiliation.

Contact info: David Moore, co-founder of Sludge, a nonprofit newsroom
(917) 753-3462
readsludge@gmail.com
http://readsludge.com

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

David Moore

communications.subject: Request for Public Records - DSHS Request ID# 202601 PRR 258
communications.communication:
Request for Public Records - DSHS Request ID# 202601 PRR 258

This email responds under the Public Records Act, Ch. 42.56 RCW, to your public records request on behalf of the Department of Social and Health Services (DSHS). We received your request on January 9, 2026 (see attached). Please use the above request ID number when contacting us about this request.

Please note, DSHS did not receive your requests that you indicate were sent by email on December 9, 2025 and December 31, 2025, directed to Adolfo Capestany.  You stated that you received an "error" message when submitting your request(s) via email to Adolfo Capestany at adolfo.capestany@dshs.wa.gov<mailto:adolfo.capestany@dshs.wa.gov> due to the email being undeliverable.  A possible explanation is that Adolfo Capestany was no longer a DSHS employee at the time of your request(s), his last date of employment with DSHS was November 1, 2025.

You asked for copies of the following records:

For the time period December 9, 2025, through January 9, 2026, requesting data on the employers in the state of Washington with more than 100 workers receiving benefits from the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP), commonly referred to as food assistance. Specifically, I request the following information, if available:
*         The name of the employer
*         The total number of the employer's workers who are currently receiving SNAP benefits
*         The time period this data covers (e.g., the most recent quarter or year for which data is available)

For your reference, to assist you in the future, to submit a request for DSHS public records send your request by mail, email or phone to:

DSHS Public Disclosure
Office of Information Governance
PO Box 45135
Olympia WA 98504-5135
Phone: (360) 902-8484
Fax: (360) 902-7855
Email: DSHSPublicDisclosure@dshs.wa.gov

Our office will send your request to the part(s) of DSHS that may have the records you seek.  Due to current workloads, the number of other pending requests and the scope of your request, we estimate it could take up to 35 (thirty-five) business days to find, review, and copy available responsive records, to the extent records exist. On or about March 9, 2026, you may anticipate a response from DSHS. Records may be produced in installments as appropriate.

If you have any questions, please contact our office.

Connie White, CDP(r)   / Public Records Administrator
Information Governance / Office of the Secretary
Washington State Department of Social and Health Services
(O) 360-902-8484 / (Fax)  360-902-7855
DSHSPublicDisclosure@dshs.wa.gov<mailto:DSHSPublicDisclosure@dshs.wa.gov>

[DSHS Logo with three people outlined by the state of Washington.]
Office of the Secretary
Office of Information Governance
Washington State Department
of Social and Health Services

title: Face recognition agreements, training, and use materials (Orange County Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Face recognition agreements, training, and use materials (Orange County Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

This is a public records request being submitted under California Public Records Act.

I hereby request the following records related to this agency’s use of any face recognition platform, including but not limited to any system over which this agency does not maintain primary ownership but to which members of the agency have access or any platform that enables face recognition:

1. AGREEMENTS and CONTRACTS
Any and all agreements (including related amendments, attachments, and exhibits) related to the acquisition and use of this "software," algorithm, or program.

2. INSTRUCTIONAL MATERIALS and TRAINING
All instructional materials, presentations and presentation materials and other guidance on the use of "the software." Responsive materials include recorded video and audio, PowerPoint files, prepared remarks, and slides. This also includes training material governing the use, sharing, or access to the "software" or any data related to or collected by the face recognition software/technology, including the legal standard that is required before using the technology.

3. PRIVACY ASSESSMENTS
A copy of any data retention guidelines or policies or other materials evaluating or guiding the security of the "software" or the privacy of the data involved.

4. USE POLICIES 
Please provide a copy of any policy directives, guidance documents, memoranda, training materials, or similar records governing the use and function of this technology for any law enforcement purpose. Responsive materials include all those related to data retention, permissible and impermissible use, and security standards.

5. USE LOGS
All logs documenting the use of and searched conducted using the face recognition system for the period from August 1, 2025 through February 23, 2026. 

This is a request being made in the public interest and not for commercial motives.

If your office anticipates that it will need to charge fees for the processing of this request, please inform me of the estimated costs to fulfill this request in advance of fully processing this request.

Please provide these materials to me electronically, if possible.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response in accordance with the dictates of the public records law.

Sincerely,

Maia Ancho

title: Financial Report Request (University of Alabama)

communications.subject: Alabama Open Records Law Request: Financial Report Request (University of Alabama)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Alabama Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

1) I would like a copy of the annual NCAA MFRS report for FY2017 - FY2025. These annual reports are submitted by the athletic department and record the revenue/expenses of the department. It is an approximately 90 page document listing revenue and expenses in the athletic department by team. It is a standard annual NCAA form. The latest FY2025 report was due, 1/15/2025 so should be available. If you are unable to include this, can you please specify if you will send it to me immediately when it's available or if you want me to make a new request. That should be 9 reports combined.

2) I'd like copies of the latest contracts for your athletic director and head coaches of football, mens/womens basketball, and mens/womens ice hockey if you have those teams. That should be 6 documents if you have that many teams.

Please let me know if you have any questions. Thank you for your time.

Best,

Greg Chick

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

André Natta, a citizen of Alabama, in coordination with Greg Chick.

title: Capitola Flock Emails - Aug 2025 - Jan 2026

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Capitola Flock Emails - Aug 2025 - Jan 2026
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Please provide all emails between any email address with the domain @flocksafety.com and any City Council member, the City Manager, the Mayor, the Interim Chief of Police, the Chief of Police, the Police Administrative Captain and the Police Administrative Analyst sent between August 1, 2025 and January 26, 2026.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Syd Jones

communications.subject: Request for Public Records # 26-029
communications.communication:
Dear Syd Jones,

The City of Capitola ("City") received the Public Records Act request you submitted, received on January 27, 2026 ("Request #26-029").  A copy of your Request is attached.

The City intends to cooperate to the fullest extent possible with the Public Records Act ("PRA") (Government Code section 7920.000 et seq.). Under established California law, the City is obliged to comply with a request for a public record so long as the requester makes a specific and focused request for information, the City maintains that information in its ordinary course of business, the information is disclosable, and the record can be located with reasonable effort. The PRA provides for the inspection or copying of existing identifiable public records; it does not compel the City to respond directly to questions or create new records, lists, or reports in response to a request.

The City has determined that it possesses responsive records to your request. The City is collecting and reviewing records for responsiveness and privilege.  Review is ongoing.  The City will endeavor to provide responsive records on or before March 6, 2026.

If you have any questions regarding the City's response or would like to discuss your Request, please contact me at cityclerk@ci.capitola.ca.us<mailto:cityclerk@ci.capitola.ca.us>.

Sincerely,


[cid:image001.jpg@01DC969C.A4D8D2D0]
Julia Gautho, CMC
City Clerk

communications.subject: RE: Request for Public Records # 26-029
communications.communication:
Good Morning,

The City of Capitola ("City") is in receipt of the Public Records Act request you submitted, received on January 27, 2026 ("Request"), seeking "all emails between any email address with the domain @flocksafety.com and any City Councilmember, the City Manager, the Mayor, the Interim Chief of Police, the Chief of Police, the Police Administrative Captain and the Police Administrative Analyst sent between August 1, 2025 and January 26, 2026."

The City wishes to cooperate to the fullest extent possible with the Public Records Act. Under established California law, the City of obliged to comply with a request for a public record so long as the requester makes a specific and focused request for information, that information is maintained by the City in its ordinary course of business, the information is disclosable, and the record can be located with reasonable effort. The Public Records Act provides for the inspection or copying of existing identifiable public records; it does not compel the City to create new records, lists, privilege logs, or reports in response to a request.

On February 6, 2026, the City determined that it possesses records responsive to your Request.  The City now provides records responsive to your Request attached to this correspondence.

With this letter and production, the City Considers your Request closed.  If you have any questions regarding the City's response or would like to discuss your Request, please contact me at cityclerk@ci.capitola.ca.us.
